title: Reducing gunshot victimization in high-risk social networks through direct and spillover effects

meta_data.Data_Name: Chicago Police Department arrests administrative records
meta_data.Data_summary: Individual-level arrest records used to build a co-arrest social network by linking pairs of people arrested together and to derive covariates such as prior arrests and charges for violence-related and weapons-related offenses.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: To measure exposure to the programme through social ties we used data on 868607 arrests in the jurisdiction of the Chicago Police Department from the period 2007 to 2017, Location: Methods Network exposure mapping, Confidence: high

meta_data.Data_Name: Intervention programme assignment and compliance records for call-in meetings
meta_data.Data_summary: Administrative programme records identifying individuals invited to the intervention meetings and whether they attended, used to define treatment assignment and compliance status for seeds and to set peer exposure start dates based on the seed invitation date.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: We conducted a quasi-experimental evaluation using participation data and administrative data, Location: Main, Confidence: high

meta_data.Data_Name: Gunshot victimization administrative outcome data linked to seeds and peers
meta_data.Data_summary: Individual-level records indicating whether and when a person experienced gunshot victimization, used as the primary outcome measured in the two years following invitation or potential spillover exposure and as pre-intervention covariates capturing prior victimizations.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: we compared victimization outcomes in the two years following invitation to the programme, Location: Main, Confidence: medium

meta_data.Data_Name: Paper-level de-identified replication dataset generated by the authors
meta_data.Data_summary: De-identified replication data derived from the original restricted administrative and programme data and used to reproduce the analyses reported in the study.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: De-identified replication data generated and analysed in this study are available from the corresponding author upon request, Location: Extra info Data availability, Confidence: high
